frameworks.name: recurrent neural network (supervised RNN)
frameworks.one_line: trained RNN as model of LPFC population dynamics during retro-cued working memory

frameworks.name: neural population geometry (subspace coding)
frameworks.one_line: items live in orthogonal vs parallel population subspaces across delays

frameworks.name: error-minimisation / supervised learning
frameworks.one_line: subspace transitions emerge from gradient training on the cued-recall task

core_question: How can the orthogonal-to-parallel subspace transformation seen in macaque LPFC during retro-cueing be learnt through error minimisation?
key_assumptions: LPFC neural geometry during retro-cueing can be modelled by a supervised RNN
an equivalent cued-recall task with conjunctive colour-location stimuli captures the relevant computation
representational geometry (orthogonal vs parallel subspaces) is the right level of description
manipulations: duration of post-cue maintenance before readout
task structure with location retro-cue selecting one of two conjunctive items
key_findings: RNNs trained on the cued-recall task spontaneously developed the orthogonal-to-parallel geometry observed in macaque LPFC
in pre-cue delay items occupied independent subspaces; in post-cue delay prioritised items rotated into a common subspace
parallel geometry only emerged when the cued information had to be maintained for several cycles before readout
interpretations: the orthogonal-to-parallel transformation can be learnt purely through error minimisation on the task
parallel geometry may confer robustness to maintained working-memory representations
